defects.observation: The Golden Gate Bridge checkpoint and its complete 17-by-17 local column field resolve to water at y=54 with no standable representative surface.
defects.qualification: This does not yet distinguish a landmark-coordinate error from a bridge-generation omission.
defects.repair: Audit the bridge geometry and checkpoint together; change the recipe coordinate only if the generated bridge is present elsewhere.
defects.expectedBenefit: Restores a recognizable, standable regional anchor without city-specific logic in the compiler.
defects.validation: Rerun the checkpoint field and require a non-water headroom-bearing surface within the declared landmark tolerance.

defects.observation: The Brooklyn Bridge Manhattan Approach checkpoint and its complete 17-by-17 local column field resolve to water at y=-53 with no standable representative surface.
defects.qualification: This does not yet distinguish a landmark-coordinate error from a bridge-generation omission.
defects.repair: Audit approach geometry and checkpoint placement as a pair before editing generation policy.
defects.expectedBenefit: Makes the east-side bridge transition recognizable and traversable.
defects.validation: Rerun the checkpoint field and a route-aligned bridge approach inspection.

defects.observation: A survival observer at the deterministic One World Trade checkpoint died and respawned 17 times during one native Minecraft day, primarily to witch magic.
defects.qualification: This is a bounded spawn-region result under normal difficulty, not a city-wide danger rate.
defects.repair: Move the default survival arrival to a verified sheltered surface or publish a small safe-arrival envelope while preserving normal ecology outside it.
defects.expectedBenefit: Prevents immediate death loops without weakening the world-wide native ecology.
defects.validation: Repeat the full-day soak from the proposed arrival and require zero involuntary deaths while retaining nearby mob turnover.

defects.observation: After one native day, 70 of 78 nearby San Francisco entities and 48 of 55 nearby Manhattan entities were hostile.
defects.qualification: Protocol-visible entities within 128 blocks of one arrival per place; not a world-wide mob census.
defects.repair: Add an arrival-site safety audit and habitat-lighting diagnostic; do not globally disable spawning.
defects.expectedBenefit: Preserves real Minecraft ecology while preventing generated urban cavities from becoming accidental mob farms.
defects.validation: Compare hostile share, animal presence, and observer survival before and after a bounded place repair.

defects.observation: Four widely separated San Francisco checkpoints expose only two biome IDs in the sparse inspection.
defects.qualification: Sparse checkpoint evidence, not a full-world biome census.
defects.repair: Run a full biome census before deciding whether geographically informed biome assignment belongs in generation.
defects.expectedBenefit: Could improve weather, vegetation, color, and spawning variety if the coarse result is world-wide.
defects.validation: Census all generated chunks and compare biome diversity against declared place expectations.

defaults.decision: Keep cinematic, playable, and living as separate named runtime profiles rather than one maximum setting.
defaults.expectedBenefit: Makes visual range, ordinary play, and native simulation independently reproducible and avoids coupling agent work to rendering choices.
defaults.validation: Require every accepted place to pass all three profiles on the benchmark hardware class.

defaults.decision: Use creative, peaceful, view distance 32, simulation distance 10, and mob spawning off for directed tours and atlas capture.
defaults.expectedBenefit: Maximizes visible city extent and shot stability; observed median throughput was 2,156 TPS in San Francisco and 1,940.5 TPS in Manhattan.
defaults.validation: Retain the 20 TPS floor and visual checkpoint review on each new place.

defaults.decision: Use survival, normal difficulty, view distance 16, simulation distance 10, and native mobs for ordinary multiplayer.
defaults.expectedBenefit: Balances human exploration range with vanilla mechanics; the slowest observed repetition retained 19.5 times real-time headroom.
defaults.validation: Add multi-player concurrency cases before raising the max-player claim.

defaults.decision: Use survival, normal difficulty, view and simulation distance 12, native time/weather/random ticks/spawning, and no required custom ecology for living-world experiments.
defaults.expectedBenefit: Keeps Minecraft authoritative and leaves Behold-style minds as optional inhabitants; median throughput remained 463.5 TPS in San Francisco and 418 TPS in Manhattan.
defaults.validation: Repeat a full native day plus concurrency-specific entity tests for each new place.

defaults.decision: Before accepting a place, project declared landmarks, inspect complete local column fields, and verify a deterministic standable survival arrival.
defaults.expectedBenefit: Catches missing anchors and death-loop arrivals without requiring a heavyweight ontology or city-specific compiler branch.
defaults.validation: Make coordinate-bearing checkpoint evidence and a clean bounded arrival soak part of acceptance.

nonClaims: Direct landmark transects are diagnostic cross-sections, not street routes or pathfinding proofs.
One protocol observer does not establish capacity for hundreds or thousands of embodied or LLM-powered agents.
Accelerated tick throughput does not scale linearly with agent count, loaded regions, pathfinding, or inference load.
Sparse checkpoint biome observations are not a complete biome census.
Water at a bridge checkpoint does not by itself prove that the bridge geometry is absent.
The benchmark does not couple place artifacts to Behold characters, looms, or world lifecycles.
